action_items.task: Write the L4 leveling recommendation for JC and send it to the comp team so they can model the offer.
action_items.evidence_quote: Vik: I'll write it up and send the leveling rec to comp by tomorrow... yeah, tomorrow.

action_items.task: Backchannel with her former colleague at Volta who worked with JC two years ago.
action_items.evidence_quote: Sara: I could backchannel. Vik: yeah, would you? Sara: yeah I'll ping her tonight.

action_items.task: Send the rejection email to the other final candidate (KP).
action_items.evidence_quote: Vik: can you send it out? Mei: yeah, I'll get it out. Probably tomorrow. Vik: tomorrow good? Mei: yeah tomorrow.

non_commitments.why_not: Mei said it 'depends on comp turnaround. I can have a draft ready basically as soon as I have numbers' — explicitly conditional on an external trigger, no fixed deadline.

non_commitments.text: Tom drafting a new coding question for the interview loop
non_commitments.why_not: Tom said 'I could draft something at some point', Vik replied 'yeah let me know if you want help' — vague, no commitment, no deadline.

non_commitments.text: Reaching out to the ML candidate Tom met at the conference
non_commitments.why_not: Vik said 'hmm yeah maybe. Let me think about whether that role is open. Probably not this quarter.' — explicit hedge.
Internal scheduling, not a tracked deliverable.
